title: Patterson's Portuguese Coronation
title_candidates: 168 and Counting: A New TEG Benchmark
Seve, Sand and a Stableford Masterclass
theme: John Patterson turns a sextuple-bogey opening into the highest Stableford total in TEG history, while David Mullin strolls to the Jacket and Stuart Neumann is glued to the bottom from Friday onwards.
foreshadow: Patterson's R1 ten at the 4th (b04) — a wobble that, in hindsight, looks like the last bad hour of his tournament
Mullin's quiet, metronomic +15 on R1 (b07 context) — the Jacket race essentially ended on day one
Neumann's R1 back-nine bleed (b44) — the Spoon was sealed before anyone realised there was a race

competitions.name: Trophy (Stableford)
competitions.how: Patterson opened with 38 pts despite a sextuple bogey at the 4th (b04) and trailed Jon Baker by one. He seized the lead for good at R2 H1 (b58) and detonated the tournament with a 49-pt round at Praia D'El Rey — the second-best round in TEG history (b02) — built on a 17-point burst through 9-13 (b13) and a closing 14 across 15-18 (b26). A solid 34 in the tricky R3 kept him three clear; in R4 Alex Baker briefly drew level at the 14th (b45) before Patterson's 47-pt finish carried him to 168 — a new all-time record (b01).

competitions.name: Green Jacket (Gross)
competitions.how: Mullin took outright gross lead at R1 H3 (b81), surrendered it briefly to Jon Baker mid-round (b88), reclaimed level at the 15th (b86) and held outright by the 18th (b83) — the decisive takeover. From there it was a procession: +15, +14, +22, +16, the lead extending from 2 to 7 to 7 to 10. A R3 quintuple at the 10th (b11) and a R4 opening eight (b25) were the only real ripples on a serene runaway to +67, by 10 from Jon Baker (b07).

competitions.name: Wooden Spoon
competitions.how: Neumann was last after holes 1 and 2 only briefly before Alex Baker's 9 at the 2nd (b61) shuffled the order. He dropped to the bottom at R1 H11 (b62), briefly escaped, then sank for good with a quintuple bogey 8 at the 15th (b63) and a 16-shot bleed across 14-18 (b44). He never climbed off the bottom rung again across all four rounds, finishing on 127 pts (b19) — despite genuinely promising patches like the 16-point burst through 11-15 in R4 (b18).

headline_candidates: Seve's Last Design, and Six Players Lost in It
Patterson Takes a Ten and Still Sleeps Second
Mullin Quietly Files the Only Sensible Card
rounds.chosen_headline: Seve's Last Design, and Six Players Lost in It
rounds.angle: A blowup-strewn opening at Royal Óbidos sets the table: Mullin gets the gross lead, Patterson survives a sextuple, Neumann sinks on the back nine.

headline_candidates: Patterson Builds the Mountain at Praia D'El Rey
A Round for the Record Books
rounds.angle: Patterson's 49-point round — the second-best in TEG history — turns a one-shot deficit into a ten-shot lead and effectively settles the Trophy.

headline_candidates: Three Quadruples and a Lead Intact
rounds.angle: A messy 34 from the leader — three quadruples on one card — trims the cushion to three, but no one in the chasing pack manages to capitalise.

headline_candidates: 168: A Number for the Record Book
Patterson Closes With 47, Mullin Cruises, Neumann Anchored
rounds.chosen_headline: 168: A Number for the Record Book
rounds.angle: Alex Baker draws level on Stableford at the 14th, but Patterson's 47-pt finish takes him to the highest TEG Stableford total ever; Mullin lifts the Jacket by ten, Neumann claims the Spoon.

players.arc: Survives a R1 sextuple, then plays the best stretch of golf of his life — a 49-point Saturday — to set a new all-time TEG record of 168.
Quietly metronomic from R1 H3 onwards; led the Jacket race wire-to-wire and won by ten without ever needing to break sweat.
Two personal-best 44-pt rounds, a brief share of the Trophy lead at R4 H14 — and a sackful of blowups (the 11 at R1 H14 chief among them) that kept him from going closer.
The early Trophy leader and eventual gross runner-up; a hot R3 start (16 pts in 5 holes) couldn't undo a R2 H14 quadruple or his R4 17th.
The blowup of the week: sextuples at R1 H15, R2 H9 and R4 H7, plus a 9 on the opening tee — a personal anthology of Iberian disaster.
Bottom from R1 H15 to the final hole; bright patches (the 16-point R4 burst) couldn't outrun the cold stretches that defined his week.

venue_notes: Frame as TEG's 3rd visit to the Lisbon Coast (2016), a familiar return alternating Seve Ballesteros' final design at Royal Óbidos (R1 is the 2nd TEG round there; R3 the 3rd) with the parkland-into-dunes drama of Praia D'El Rey (R2 the 3rd visit, R4 the 4th). Lean on Seve's water features for the R1/R3 blowups and the coastal back nine at Praia D'El Rey for R2/R4 colour.
